E-girls

E-girls是於2011年以EXILE的妺分組合Dream、Happiness和Flower為中心所構成的girls entertainment project，並於同年12月28日以單曲「Celebration!」正式出道。
2017年6月5日宣布E-girls改制並組成E.G.family。7月15日 - 7月16日，隨著『E-girls LIVE 2017 〜E.G. EVOLUTION〜』Dream活動正式完結，而由3個組合為中心的project E-girls亦以11人繼續活動。

Amiは、02年に「dream」に加入。11年からはE－girlsのメンバーとして活躍し、15年にソロデビュー。E－girls卒業後もソロ歌手として活動し、先月までは「E．G．family」として全国ツアーにも参加していた。

半田氏は、15年から建築家志望のイケメンとして「テラスハウス」に出演。当時、彼女がいることを明かして参加し、誠実なキャラで「半さん」の愛称で親しまれた。現在もインスタグラムのフォロワー数は17・5万人を超えるなど人気だ。

半田氏は、15年から建築家志望のイケメンとして「テラスハウス」に出演。当時、彼女がいることを明かして参加し、誠実なキャラで「半さん」の愛称で親しまれた。現在もインスタグラムのフォロワー数は17・5万人を超えるなど人気だ。

Amiの所属事務所は「プライベートは本人に任せています」としている。

I saw E-girls live a couple years ago at Yokohama Arena which was absolutely incredible and unforgettable. They’ve been strutting their stuff since they were kids so don’t sell them short😊At the end of the last encore they bowed onstage soaking up the applause 👏🏻 They received a thunderous ovation. On top of being a great musician 👩🏻‍🎤 Sayaka is also an avid runner 🏃🏻‍♀️ A couple of years ago she took part in a five hundred metre race where she came third. She rose to the challenge of passing the runner 🏃🏻‍♀️ who was ahead of her earlier, ran for all she was worth and dived over the finish line 🏁 Loads of fans congratulated her on having won the race as she held up her trophy 🏆 The race was the opportunity for all the best female runners 🏃🏻‍♀️ in Tokyo to strut their stuff. It was truly memorable. Last week my Japanese friend Keita asked if I wanted to join him for lunch at his house 🏡 I replied in the affirmative. We washed two boxes of cheese salad 🥗 and left the salad to dry out before consuming it. In this way the cheese salad 🥗 contains more nutrients than when you consume it right after opening the lid. After lunch I said “I’ll wash 🧼 you dry” He came to the kitchen and stood beside me drying up the cutlery and utensils while I washed. Since it’s best to dry your clothes out the day before by hanging them on the clothesline I went ahead and helped him do that. Keita is a marketing manager at Hilton. He uses photoshop every day since it’s worth its weight in gold in digital marketing. I admire his ambition and dogged determination to succeed. He never gets sidetracked by personal issues. He enjoys participating in debates. Last week he wiped the floor with all opposition during a debate about the existence of god. Amazing. His brother-in-law is a travel agent who earns a basic salary of eight thousand eight hundred dollars per month plus a twenty five percent commission on every deal he makes so he doesn’t work by commission only. He’s a fan of Jacqueline Wong. Have you heard of the sex scandal involving her and Andy Hui? She was having an affair with Andy who’s married to Sammi. It caused a scandal which ended Jacqueline’s career overnight. They were definitely an item. As a result many people think it’s time for her to hang up her spurs. Her friend Charles hung up his spurs several years ago because of a sex scandal.

I hear E-girls will have perform at Saitama Super Arena in the near future. If so I’ll need to get front row tickets 🎫 Last week I joined my Japanese friend Keita on a run 🏃🏻 along the promenade which felt relaxing. His father is a physics professor 👨🏻‍🏫 who along with his team are solving a notoriously difficult math question for all they’re worth. Hopefully they’ll find an answer to it soon. Besides physics he’s also an avid football fan. His favourite team Barcelona is currently top of the league whereas Milan is bottom of the league. Rome is lying fourth in the league this year. The league championship is the opportunity for the best teams in the league to strut their stuff. His sister is a saleswoman 👩🏻‍💼 but she feels she’s made the wrong career choice. I told her “why not wipe the slate clean and start over in a different career?” Keita too suggested her to study a degree course at university and start with a clean slate. She blames Keita for convincing her to be a saleswoman 👩🏻‍💼 I said “let’s just wipe the slate clean. It’s over.” His cousin is planning on doing a PhD since today a master’s degree 📜 isn’t worth the paper it’s printed on let alone a bachelor degree 📜 Last month she nearly got swindled out of thousands of dollars. I told her that person was a scammer who was trying to swindle money 💰 from her. The contract isn’t worth the paper it’s written on. Fortunately she listened to me. Otherwise the scammer would’ve swindled her out of loads of money. By the way yesterday she dropped a dumbbell 💪🏼 and put a dent in my chair. I said I’d wipe her slate clean if she fixed the dent in the chair 😆His auntie is a professional footballer. She’s so focused during training that she never goes off on a sidetrack. The Japanese culture is very different to ours. We enjoy yum cha while they enjoy drinking green tea 🍵 and watching football at bars soaking up the atmosphere.
I’m soaking in a jacuzzi and there’s a gym beside it of which you can see the inside clearly. I saw a very slim guy lift a light two pound dumbbell 💪🏼 for all he was worth but still couldn’t complete the exercise🤣I enjoy working out at the gym. I know my personal trainer is worth his salt because I’m making good progress with my physique. By the way happy birthday to Keita! I’m sure your friend will join me in wishing you a very happy birthday 🎊🎂

I've assigned three and a half hours for practicing their songs on my Tuba, contrabassoon, trombone and cornet. I remember seeing E-girls live at Yokohama Arena which was absolutely incredible and unforgettable. They've been strutting their stuff since they were kids so don't sell them short. At the end of the last encore they bowed onstage soaking up the applause. They received a thunderous ovation. On top of being a great musician Yurino is also an avid runner. She took part in a three thousand metre race where she came second. She rose to the occasion, rin for all she was worth and dived over the finish line passing the runner who was ahead of her earlier. Her family and lots of fans congratulated her for having won the race as she held the trophy up. The race was the opportunity for all the best female runners in osaka to strut their stuff. It was remarkable. I dined at a ramen restaurant nearby after the concert. I remember the table was sticky maybe because the staff forgot to wipe it up so I asked them to wipe it up. I spilled a bowl of miso soup all over the table and on my shirt so I had to wipe the spilt soup up lol. I had a nice chat with a lady sitting at a nearby table who told me once upon a time a whole village in tokyo was wiped out by a super gigantic earthquake. Fascinating. When I finished my meal I wiped my mouth on my sleeve lol and a piece of tissue. There's an elegant cafe nearby of which the interior and exterior are artistically and exquisitely designed. My friend recently spilt his cappuccino all over his work as well as down his tie and on his trousers so he ended up wiping up this mess. I enjoy drinking coffee and chatting with the baristas there so much that I sometimes make it worth their while. My friend Fuyuki has opened a gym. So far it's been a great success. The excellent quality gym and shower equipment is a decisive factor in the success of his gym. Of course customer service also plays a key role in the company's success. He asked whether I could find a good manager for him. I said "I can't make any promises but I'll see what I can do." My friend Tomiko has an interview for a marketing director position in the company tomorrow. Fuyuki laid heavy stress on the need for leadership qualities. He also stressed that clear communication skill is a key factor in the outcome of the interview. It stresses her out. I said "Try not to stress too much about it. Just put your mind at rest." My brother too said "dont stress yourself out. Rest easy. Set your mind at ease. Its going to be ok." Last week we had supper at her place. Her younger brother got Japaness homework to do. He said "I've got to do part A but i dont have to do part B." I said "I'll leave you doing it." He soaks up new vocabularies and grammar patterns very quickly. In a japanese class he soaks up everything thr teacher says. When "Fukushima" is a Japanese word the stress is on the second, third and last syllables. But when it's an english word you should only stress the third syllable. When he reads a novel he's like a sponge and soaks up the story. If he encounters computer problems he'll find Sam who's an expert and soak up the advice he offers. Her elder brother has a viola exam next week. After chatting with him for a while I said "I'd better leave you to get on with it" My brother too said "I'd better leave you to practise your pieces" Some milk spilt down the chair onto the floor so she soaked it up with a dishcloth. Her elder sis booked a massage session. She asked "had i better leave my wallet on the nightstand?" I said "you'd better not leave it there during your session as someone might steal it" My bro said "hadn't you better leave it in your locker so that its safe?" Her younger sis went to meditate on her room after supper so we left her to herself. I can swim after a fashion. Tomiko is a swimming champion who easily outshines me in any swimming styles.

今日E-girls嘅活動宣布

武部繼續unit活動！

之後會上載官方新聞係度。

この度E-girlsは、来年2020年いっぱいをもって約9年間の活動に終止符を打ち、それぞれの未来に向かい活動していくこととなりました。

佐藤晴美、坂東希、石井杏奈、山口乃々華はそれぞれ役者やモデルを、Happinessは新たに88risingとタッグを組み本格的に世界進出を目指し、武部柚那はアフロジャックが世界8都市で開催したオーディション合格メンバーと新たなユニットを結成、鷲尾伶菜はソロアーティストとしてデビューいたします。

突然の発表で驚かせてしまったと思いますが、この場を借りてメンバー11人の気持ちを皆様にお伝えさせていただきたいと思います。

2011年にE-girlsの活動がスタートし、私達がどんな状況にあろうと、いつもどんな時でも皆様が応援してくださったおかげで、夢にまで見た景色をたくさん見ることができました。

E-girlsは多くの出会いと別れを繰り返しながらも、メンバー同士で支え合い、そしてスタッフの皆さんと手を取り合い8年間頑張ってきました。

でも、いつからかメンバーそれぞれの中で、人生の次なる目標や夢が芽生えるようになり、その想いをメンバー同士で何度も話し合ってきました。
全員が同じ気持ちになれず意見がすれ違うこともありましたが、長い時間をかけてメンバー全員でたくさん考え、今回の決断をさせていただきました。

人生の中での大切な時間や青春をE-girlsと共にし、メンバーはもちろん、応援してくださる皆様には一生忘れることのない財産をいただきました。

心から感謝の気持ちでいっぱいです。
本当に本当にありがとうございます。

来年の「E-girls PERFECT LIVE 2011→2020」が最後のツアーになりますので、2020年末までの一年間で、皆様への感謝や自分達の想いをメンバー全員で精一杯伝えたいと思います。

引き続き応援よろしくお願いします。

i was watching television and got sidetracked into browsing this forum. I saw E-Girls live twice with my friends at Saitama Super Arena which was marvelous and unforgettable. I remember we were late. I said "look at the time. The concert will have begun" It was twenty to nine. The concert would've started by then. It was a worthwhile concert that was compelling enough to see again. E-Girls have been strutting their stuff since they were kids do don't sell them short. At the end of the last encore they bowed onstage soaking up the applause. They met with a standing ovation and thunderous applause. The group and the dancers and acrobats who were invited to perform alongside E-Girls gave a superb performance. The stage and lighting design, the choreography and the glow sticks were magnificent and surpassed our expectations. E-Girls is very career-minded. My cousin who's also career-oriented is planning on having them endorse his company's products but he'll have to make it financially worthwhile for them. They have a worthwhile job and career. I regard music performing as a worthwhile career. On top of being an incredible musician, Sayaka is an avid runner. She took part in a five hundred meter race where she came first. She rose to the occasion, ran for all she was worth and dived across the finish line passing the runners who were ahead of her earlier. Loads of fans and her family congratulated her on winning the race as she held up the trophy. The race was the opportunity for all the world's best female runners to strut their stuff. It was truly memorable. Yesterday I assigned five hours for practising their songs on my saxophone, piccolo, flute, oboe, cor anglais (english horn), clarinet, E-flat clarinet, bass clarinet, bassoon and contrabassoon. I practised with my friends who played glockenspiel, xylophone, vibraphone, marimba, chimes, celesta and timpani (kettle-drum), all of which have definite pitch. Adding these instruments makes the music more rhythmic. Saxophone, clarinet and bass clarinet are single reed instruments whereas oboe, cor anglais, bassoon and contrabassoon are double reed instruments. Flute is a reedless instrument.

After I saw their concert, I dined in a nearby ramen restaurant. I remember tje table was sticky maybe because the staff forgot to wipe it up so I asked them to wipe it up. Later I spilt a bowl of miso soup all over the table and my trousers do I had to wipe the spilled soup up. I had a friendly chat with a lady sitting at a nearby table who told me once upon a time a whole village in tokyo was wiped out by a super gigantic earthquake which was fascinating to hear. When I finished my meal I wiped my mouth on my sleeve lol and a few pieces of tissues. There's an elegant cafe nearby of which the interior and the exterior are artistically and exquisitely designed. My friend spilt his cappuccino all over his work and down his tie the other day so he ended up wiping up this mess. I enjoy drinking coffee and chatting with the baristas there so much that I sometimes make it worth their while. It's a fair bet that the Japanese yen will continue falling in the next four weeks so you might as well go to Tokyo on vacation this year. If so I heartily recommend that you take the airport express at IFC. It's very easy. Once you're at the ifc exit where you can head straight for Queens road central, enter ifc, pass the escalator in front of you and keep walking until you come to another escalator. Go down it, turn left and go straight ahead it's there. If you wanna travel to Shinjuku go through the gate, go down the escalator and look at the signs. You'll see which side is bound for Shinjuku.  If you wanna have a coffee at J.Cafe take the lift to level three go straight down the path and pass several shops until you come to an intersection. Turn right and it's there. If you want to take the elevator to a higher floor, turn left at the intersection and pass several shops until you see pure fitness on your left. Enter pure fitness and the lift is there. However if you ask me if Australia is a great place to go for your holidays don't bet on it. There's nothing worth doing there.

My friend John practises E-Girls' songs on his bassoon six hours a day in pursuit of gaining a distinction in his graded exam. He's single-minded in his pursuit of his goal. I've never met anyone so ambitious and single-minded. A trombonist with whom he's rehearsing is also very single-minded and dedicated in pursuit of studying a degree course at a conservatory. They both have something common which is the single-minded pursuit of their goals. A friend of mine Sarah is planning on starting a tv network. She's already fired up and ruthless and dedicated in pursuit of profit. She wants to have one channel dedicated to basketball and another channel dedicated to combat sports. She's devoted to making it the best dedicated sports network in the world. Sayaka's brother has a job interview tomorrow. I said "I'm rooting for you. I'm there for you. You got this. I've got your back." My girlfriend too said "I support you. It's gonna be ok" His girlfriend has geography exams next week so she's preoccupied with revision. I said "you're almost there. I believe in you" My girlfriend also said "you got this. I'm there for you" When her professor was tutoring her he said "that's coming along nicely. You're learning fast. You're getting better every day. You've almost mastered that. Keep up the good work" Her tutor also said "now you've got the hang of it. You're on the right track now. You're doing fine. Keep it up" This morning Sarah got off at the wrong stop and had to wait for another train. I asked "how on earth did you miss your stop?" She said "I'm sorry. What can I say?" I complimented her on her cornwt skills. She said "yes what can I say" The current situation is very bad. I then said "well yes what can I say?" We aren't able to fix the situation.
Before attending E-Girls' concert last month, I was shocked when I saw a car crash on the street. My brother said "I can imagine" My cousin said "I'm sure you were"
